title: Cisco Secure Client / AnyConnect Posture Agent Heartbeat and Version Drift

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 5.3 — anti-malware mechanisms must be operational. Agent heartbeat evidences operational status.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.54 alert log + monthly agent-coverage report.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.7 — protection against malware via active agent.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a lab endpoint, stop the Cisco Secure Client posture service. Wait 8 days (or simulate by changing system clock). UC-17.1.54 fires for the endpoint with `days_since_heartbeat > 7`.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Restart the agent. Heartbeat resumes. Alert clears.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Posture* category — codes `15050` (posture agent heartbeat), `15051` (posture agent version reported), `15052` (posture agent missing/timeout), `15053` (posture agent unsupported version), `15054` (posture agent stale config). Endpoint inventory feed from ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/endpoint` polled hourly. Fields: `endpoint_mac`, `agent_version`, `agent_state`, `last_heartbeat`. Cisco Secure Endpoint API is the parallel feed.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (15050, 15051, 15052, 15053))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:endpoint_inventory")
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_seen, latest(agent_version) as version, latest(agent_state) as state by endpoint_mac
| eval days_since_heartbeat = round((now() - last_seen) / 86400, 1)
| where days_since_heartbeat > 7 OR state=="timeout" OR like(version, "4.10%") OR like(version, "4.9%")
description: Detects endpoints whose posture agent (Cisco Secure Client / AnyConnect) hasn't sent a heartbeat for >7 days OR is running a deprecated/unsupported version. Both conditions break the posture trust loop.
value: Without an active posture agent, ISE has no way to validate endpoint compliance — it falls back to a default posture decision, typically less restrictive. Detecting agent absence at scale highlights the deployment gap (devices not yet enrolled, devices retired but still in inventory, or devices the install team missed).
implementation: Confirm *Posture* category at INFO. Endpoint-inventory poller every 1h. Schedule the search daily at 06:00 UTC.
visualization: (1) Single value: endpoints with stale heartbeat. (2) Bar chart: agent version distribution. (3) Top-N: device-types missing agents.
knownFalsePositives: (a) **Devices on extended leave** — Employees on leave may have endpoints offline >7 days. Tag known-extended-offline. (b) **Decommissioned devices** — Old devices still in ISE endpoint inventory. Periodic cleanup needed.
mitreAttack: N/A (agent fleet health — measures AnyConnect posture agent heartbeat, no attack mapping)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Secure Client / AnyConnect deployed to endpoints.
• ISE *Posture* category at INFO.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Validate event flow.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule daily.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Posture Agent Coverage":
• Single value: endpoints with stale heartbeat.
• Version distribution.
• Top-N missing.

Monthly review: agent-coverage report.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **High count of stale heartbeats** — Mass-decommission scenario; clean inventory.
• **All on old version** — Agent-update mechanism broken.
• **Heartbeat events absent** — *Posture* category at WARN.
grandmaExplanation: Each company computer runs a small program that tells ISE 'I'm OK'. We watch for computers that have stopped saying 'I'm OK' for over a week — they may be off, retired, or compromised.
